what: `tools/deepeval_gate.py` is a Phase-3 Python script that runs the DeepEval library's pytest-native `assert_test()` API to assert LLM-output quality against configured metric thresholds — faithfulness >= 0.8 and answer relevancy >= 0.7 — and fail the build if any threshold is not met. It is the implementation behind Requirement 30 / REQ-EVAL-001 (Eval-Gating in CI). It is intended to run as a REQUIRED CI status check named `deepeval-gate` that gates merge. (design.md:149; requirements.md:482-484; tasks.md:975)

open_gaps.gap: No CI workflow file is defined for the deepeval-gate check. Every other REQUIRED CI check has an explicit workflow + a task to create it: coverage-gate.yml (task 14.1), formal-verification.yml (task 40.1), secrets-scan.yml (task 40.2), audit-chain-verify.yml (task 40.3), zap-baseline.yml (task 56). deepeval_gate.py has the Python module (task 55) but NO .github/workflows/deepeval-gate.yml and no register-as-required-check subtask. The 'REQUIRED CI status check at merge' claim is therefore unwired.
open_gaps.fix: Add a task 40.4 (or subtask 55.1) 'Create .github/workflows/deepeval-gate.yml running pytest tools/deepeval_gate.py and register deepeval-gate as a REQUIRED status check in the GitHub repository ruleset; document in docs/github-ruleset.md', mirroring task 40.3.

open_gaps.gap: deepeval-gate is omitted from the canonical required-status-checks list. tasks.md:270 enumerates the required checks as formal-verification, coverage-gate, secrets-scan, audit-chain-verify + human reviewer — and does NOT include deepeval-gate (nor zap-baseline). So the single documented source of truth for the GitHub ruleset contradicts requirements.md:484 / design.md:149, which call deepeval-gate REQUIRED at merge.
open_gaps.fix: Update the docs/github-ruleset.md required-checks list at tasks.md:270 to include deepeval-gate (and zap-baseline), so all four P3 required gates are enumerated in one place.

open_gaps.gap: The 0.8/0.7 thresholds have no owner and are not in the Requirement-20 threshold registry. Req 20.2 mandates that ALL numeric thresholds be configurable DEFAULTs requiring explicit operator override, and Req 20.3 registers cost budget, reasoning-loop K, retry budget — but NOT the eval metrics. They appear only as 'e.g.' example values (requirements.md:483), so they are neither authoritative defaults nor owned/overridable.
open_gaps.fix: Add the eval thresholds to the Requirement-20 threshold registry (requirements.md:356) with an explicit OWNER (e.g., the quality owner from Req 7) and an operator-override path, and drop the 'e.g.' so 0.8/0.7 become the authoritative defaults rather than illustrations.

open_gaps.gap: No eval input is specified: the spec never says WHAT LLM output deepeval_gate.py evaluates, where the test cases / golden dataset come from, or which agent output is the actual_output / retrieval_context. faithfulness and answer-relevancy are RAG-style metrics requiring (input, actual_output, retrieval_context) tuples; none is sourced. The WHEN-guard 'a CI run includes LLM output evaluation' (requirements.md:483) has no defined producer.
open_gaps.fix: Specify the eval dataset/source in design.md (e.g., a golden-set fixture under tests/eval/ or evidence captured from verifier runs) and define what populates the DeepEval LLMTestCase fields; name the upstream component that produces the evaluable output.

open_gaps.gap: No judge/evaluator LLM is named. DeepEval's faithfulness and answer-relevancy metrics are LLM-as-judge metrics requiring an evaluator model; the spec names neither the model nor its provider, and gives no determinism/reproducibility note. This is a meta-risk for a system whose core thesis is 'completion from facts only, no LLM self-assessment can gate' (requirements.md:367) — yet this REQUIRED merge gate is itself an LLM judgment with no stated provider, seed, or flake mitigation.
open_gaps.fix: Name the evaluator model and provider, pin it, and add a reproducibility/flake policy (e.g., fixed model+temperature, retries, or a note that this gate is advisory-quality vs the deterministic gates) to reconcile with the 'no LLM gates' invariant.

open_gaps.gap: REQ-EVAL-001 has NO correctness Property and NO Z3 CHECK, and no PBT test task. It is the only P3 addition with neither a formal check nor concrete wiring (zap-baseline has a workflow+action; flagd has a service dir; omission-gate has Property 30/CHECK-13). The spec's own traceability vector claims 'every new requirement carries a Z3 check OR a named verification tool' (PRD:130) — deepeval_gate.py is a 'named tool' but the tool itself is unbuilt/unwired, so the traceability is nominal only.
open_gaps.fix: Either add an integration/smoke test task asserting deepeval-gate fails the build when a metric is below threshold (a 'verification tool' test), or explicitly document that REQ-EVAL-001 is verified by CI behavior rather than Z3, to make the traceability claim concrete.

open_gaps.gap: deepeval is not added to the dependency manifest. Task 15 pins z3-solver, hypothesis, jsonschema, psycopg2-binary, semgrep, playwright — deepeval is absent, so a Phase-3 build of tools/deepeval_gate.py would have an unpinned/undeclared dependency.
open_gaps.fix: Add deepeval (version-pinned) to the pyproject.toml/requirements.txt list in task 15, alongside the existing pins.

open_gaps.gap: Phase-vs-deferral tension. deepeval_gate.py is a Phase-3 component (design.md:149), while the PRD defers promptfoo to 'Phase 3+' as the later/parallel harness (PRD:45). Since deepeval is itself Phase 3, 'Phase 3+' is a near-meaningless deferral horizon for the alternative — it reads as 'roughly the same phase, but skip it'. Minor but it muddies the deferral rationale.
open_gaps.fix: Reword PRD:45 to defer promptfoo to a concrete later phase (e.g., 'post-v1 / Phase 5+') or to 'indefinitely deferred unless DeepEval proves insufficient', rather than 'Phase 3+', to avoid implying it ships beside deepeval in Phase 3.

open_gaps.gap: Design/tasks coverage is thin. This component gets exactly one row in the component inventory (design.md:149) with no behavioral section, no sequence in the data-flow diagrams, no entry in the Phase-0 'exact components' build table (design.md:621-636, which lists 14 spine components but not deepeval), no error-handling row (cf. spec_validator timeout at design.md:814), and no Phase-3 design narrative. Task 55 has zero subtasks (contrast task 52's 52.1-52.3 and task 40's 40.1-40.3). The component is essentially a one-line stub replicated across three files.
open_gaps.fix: Add a design.md subsection for deepeval_gate.py covering: eval input source, evaluator model, threshold config location, output/evidence artifact, CI workflow, ruleset registration, and failure/flake handling; and expand task 55 into subtasks (implement module, create workflow, register check, add smoke test, pin dependency) to match the rigor of tasks 40/52/56.

open_gaps.gap: No output artifact / audit-log wiring. Other gates either attach an Evidence_Record (perf_a11y_verifier, design.md:146) or feed the tamper-evident gate_audit_log (audit_log.py callers, design.md:144). deepeval_gate.py is a gate decision (allow/block merge) but is not listed among the audit-log producers/callers (requirements.md:460), so a REQUIRED merge-gate decision is not captured in the 'we can prove what happened' audit chain.
open_gaps.fix: Either wire the deepeval-gate pass/fail decision into tools/audit_log.append(...) (add it to the caller list at requirements.md:460) or document explicitly why CI-status-check gates are out of the gate_audit_log scope.

critic_findings.detail: The single GOVERNING INVARIANT of the entire system (design.md:7) is stated more absolutely than the draft's cited 'completion from facts only' line: 'deterministic gates — Claude Code hooks, CI, OPA — decide whether delivery is complete, computed solely from verifiable facts. Model self-assessment and probabilistic predictions only inform; they never gate.' deepeval-gate is declared a REQUIRED MERGE GATE (requirements.md:484; design.md:149) whose faithfulness/answer-relevancy metrics are LLM-as-judge computations. This is a direct CONTRADICTION between a REQUIRED gate and the architecture's top-level invariant, not merely an 'underspecified judge model' (which is how the draft's open_gaps[4…

tasks.md:717 is the single strongest piece of evidence that REQ-EVAL-001 has NO assigned Property, and the draft never cites it. Line 717 enumerates exactly which requirements Properties 25-30 cover ('includes Properties 25–29 ... authored under tasks 48–52; Property 30 — omission-declaration gate — authored under task 54') and confirms all 30 properties run with --hypothesis-seed=0. REQ-EVAL-001/task 55 appears nowhere in this enumeration. The draft's properties_and_checks cites tasks.md:980 (the property list) but misses the CI test-execution task (tasks.md:715-717) that proves no eval-gate property is wired into the PBT CI run — a stronger, independent confirmation that should be in the d…

The cross-reference table at requirements.md:505-512 is an authoritative, machine-readable property↔requirement map that the draft does not cite for this component. It maps Property 28→Req 27 (audit), Property 29→Req 24.2 (research), Property 30→Req 29/CHECK-13a/13b (omission). REQ-EVAL-001 (Req 30 the requirement) is structurally absent from this map. Note the dangerous numeric collision the draft only half-surfaces: 'Property 30' belongs to Requirement 29, while 'Requirement 30' is eval-gating with NO property — a reader could easily mis-credit Property 30 to Requirement 30. The draft should explicitly flag this Property-30-vs-Requirement-30 naming collision as an overstatement trap, since…

The draft flags the missing required-status-check registration but under-cites the canonical list. tasks.md:270 explicitly enumerates the required checks as 'formal-verification, coverage-gate, secrets-scan (task 45), audit-chain-verify (task 52), plus human PR reviewer' — and the human-reviewer smoke test (design.md:865, tests/smoke/test_repo_ruleset.py) only verifies 'requires a reviewer', NOT the required-checks set. So there is ZERO test or doc anywhere that would catch deepeval-gate's non-registration. The draft says deepeval-gate 'is NOT in the ruleset doc list' but should add that (a) the omission is shared with zap-baseline (both P3 required checks are absent from tasks.md:270), and …

Baseline-mapping inconsistency the draft does not surface. Requirement 30 maps to baseline blocks B08 (Verification engine, Req 9 / REQ-VERIFY-001..006) and B11 (Observability, Req 12 / REQ-OBS-001..005) per requirements.md:417. But neither B08 nor B11 has any verification/observability mechanism that produces or consumes LLM-output-quality evals — B08 is the structural/semantic/behavioral/security verifier and B11 is OTel telemetry. So the B08/B11 baseline mapping for eval-gating is nominal: there is no narrative in Requirement 9 or Requirement 12 that this gate extends, unlike Req 25 (also B08) which concretely extends the verifier with perf/a11y Evidence_Records. This is a traceability-qu…

The draft's dependencies[] and open_gaps reference 'the CI subgraph at design.md:54 / requirements.md:484' as if the architecture diagram contemplates deepeval-gate. It does not: the CI/Merge-Gate subgraph (design.md:50-55) contains only a generic GH_RULESET node ('required status checks') with no deepeval node, and design.md mentions 'deepeval' on exactly ONE line in the whole file (line 149). The draft's by_whom says deepeval-gate is 'nominally' registered by the ruleset, but should state plainly that the architecture diagram itself omits it — strengthening 'unwired' rather than implying the diagram references it. Minor, but the draft should not imply design.md:54 is a deepeval-specific de…

The draft's glossary finding (ears_stories row 'Requirements traceability table row 30') correctly notes no glossary defines 'faithfulness'/'answer relevancy'/'eval-gate', but undersells the contradiction with the spec's self-assessment. PRD:17 claims a '32-term glossary' as 'genuinely strong / accepted as-is', and design.md:1188 only ever discusses the EARS 'Complex' term — so three DeepEval-specific terms that gate a REQUIRED merge check are undefined while the spec asserts glossary completeness. The draft should escalate this from 'partial' to an explicit overstatement-of-coverage: the '32-term glossary' completeness claim (PRD:17) is contradicted by the undefined eval metrics that a requ…

No design-level error-handling / fail-mode row exists for deepeval_gate.py, and the draft mentions this only as part of its omnibus 'design coverage thin' gap. It deserves a standalone flag tied to the spec's own pattern: design.md:805-816 (Hook Failure Modes) and the spec_validator Z3-timeout row (design.md:814) establish that every gate with a non-trivial failure mode gets an explicit fail-closed behavior. An LLM-judge gate has obvious failure modes the spec elsewhere insists on handling: evaluator-LLM API timeout/unavailability, rate-limit, and non-determinism/flake. None is specified for deepeval-gate, so its fail-open-vs-fail-closed behavior on judge-LLM error is undefined — a REQUIRED …

The draft's 'conditional activation' behavior and criterion-2 WHEN-guard analysis is correct but misses that the guard makes the REQUIRED-check claim self-undermining. requirements.md:483 gates behavior on 'WHEN a CI run includes LLM output evaluation', yet requirements.md:484 makes deepeval-gate a REQUIRED status check at merge unconditionally. If no producer of evaluable LLM output is ever defined (the draft's open_gaps[3]), then a REQUIRED check that is conditionally a no-op is either (a) always-green vacuously (providing no gating value) or (b) blocking with nothing to evaluate — an internal tension between criterion 2's WHEN-guard and criterion 3's unconditional REQUIRED status. The dra…

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL include a DeepEval eval-gating step in CI using the pytest-native assert_test() API for LLM output quality assertions.
ears_needing_work.how: tools/deepeval_gate.py is the named implementation (design.md:149; task 55). Mechanism named (DeepEval + assert_test) but no eval inputs/test cases/dataset are specified, so 'LLM output quality assertions' has no concrete data source.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a CI run includes LLM output evaluation, THEN the eval step SHALL gate on configured metric thresholds (e.g., faithfulness >= 0.8, answer relevancy >= 0.7) using DeepEval assert_test(), failing the build if any threshold is not met.
ears_needing_work.how: design.md:149 and task 55 repeat the thresholds. But 'configured' has no owner/registry entry (not in the Req-20 threshold registry, requirements.md:354-359); thresholds appear only as 'e.g.' examples; and the WHEN-guard ('CI run includes LLM output evaluation') has no defined producer of evaluable …

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE eval step SHALL run as a REQUIRED CI status check at merge (deepeval-gate check name).
ears_needing_work.how: Stated in requirements.md:484, design.md:149, task 55. But there is NO workflow file (no .github/workflows/deepeval-gate.yml task, unlike zap-baseline task 56 / 40.2 / 40.3) and deepeval-gate is NOT in the ruleset required-checks doc list (tasks.md:270). So 'REQUIRED status check' is asserted but ne…

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL treat all numeric thresholds as configurable DEFAULTs, with changes requiring explicit operator override.
ears_needing_work.how: The 0.8/0.7 eval thresholds are numeric thresholds but are NOT registered in the threshold registry (Req 20.3 lists cost budget, reasoning-loop K, retry budget — not eval metrics). So the global 'all numeric thresholds configurable with operator override' invariant is not satisfied for this componen…

ears_needing_work.criterion: Requirement 30 Eval-Gating in CI maps to baseline blocks B08/B11 and PRD ref REQ-EVAL-001.
ears_needing_work.how: Baseline mapping present (requirements.md:417,481). No glossary term defines 'eval-gate', 'faithfulness', or 'answer relevancy' — these DeepEval-specific terms are used without definition (the spec claims a 32-term glossary, PRD:17, but none cover eval metrics).

ears_needing_work.criterion: Every new requirement carries a Z3 check or a named verification tool; the PBT suite is 30 numbered correctness properties (1-24 + 25-30).
ears_needing_work.how: REQ-EVAL-001 has NO Property number and NO Z3 CHECK. Properties 25-30 cover REQ-COV-007, REQ-STATE-005, REQ-SPEC-016, REQ-AUDIT-002, REQ-SPEC-017, REQ-SPEC-018 — Property 30 is the omission gate, not eval-gating (tasks.md:980). The Z3 harness stops at CHECK-13. So REQ-EVAL-001's only 'verification' …
